formations_blocks: Tunisia pressing with energy early on
Japan settling into possession before becoming more direct
Japan attacking with energetic, direct transitional play
adjustments: Japan increasingly bypassing buildup with long balls down the left channel
Tunisia responding with an active press after the opening minutes
phases.summary: Tunisia started the phase with a lively press and even created a shooting chance through Hannibal, but Japan quickly found a more vertical route into the game. After Kamada’s fourth-minute goal from a deflected cross, Japan stayed aggressive and direct, repeatedly targeting the space behind and around Tunisia’s back line with long balls, second balls, and dangerous deliveries.

formations_blocks: Japan packing the midfield with constant width
Tunisia stepping off and allowing space rather than pressing high
adjustments: Japan using balls over the top
Japan slowing the tempo to a 'slow-slow quick' rhythm
Japan building strategically down the left before trying to exploit room down the right
key_matchups: Saad vs Itakura on the outswinger and second ball
Ueda vs Talbi on the goal
Ueda vs Dahmen on the finish
phases.summary: Japan controlled the phase through Moriyasu’s 3-4-3, using a shape that let them congest midfield while still keeping constant width. Their threat came from speed of circulation, balls over the top and quick transitional attacks, while Tunisia’s defense was forced to retreat and step off rather than win duels high up the pitch. The decisive moment was Ueda’s 31st-minute goal, created by Tunisia giving him too much room to advance and finish low through Talbi into the far corner.

adjustments: Tunisia make two half-time substitutions: Ben Hmida on for Bronn and Gharbi on for Saad
phases.summary: The only clear tactical information in this phase is Tunisia’s half-time personnel change, with two substitutions suggesting an attempted reset after going 0-2 down. The commentary itself provides no explicit detail on formations, pressing structures, or specific player matchups beyond a late first-half free-kick and header that came to nothing.

formations_blocks: Japan technical triangle passing patterns down the right
Tunisia defensive shape improved to deny passing lanes
adjustments: Change of personnel without a change of structure
Hannibal dropping deeper to show for the ball and then driving forward with intent
Tunisia tracking runners more effectively and closing easy passing lanes
Japan becoming more patient in possession after a brief sticky patch
key_matchups: Ben Hmida vs Japan’s right-sided attack
Doan vs Skhiri (late challenge leading to a lucky escape from a caution)
phases.summary: This phase was played with Tunisia in a 5-3-2 and Japan in a 3-4-3, with no structural shift despite personnel changes. Tunisia improved defensively after the break, tracking runners and denying passing lanes, while Japan had a brief dip in control but stayed patient, especially in their right-side combinations and set-piece routines. Hannibal became more influential by dropping deeper and carrying Tunisia forward, but Japan’s superior out-of-possession work and eventual patience helped them keep Tunisia at arm’s length before Ito’s 69th-minute goal.

formations_blocks: Japan sustained attacking play with a high-commitment pressing/territorial approach
Tunisia were forced into a defending/blocking posture inside their box and around set pieces
adjustments: Japan made late substitutions: Seko and Y Suzuki on for Tomiyasu and Nakamura (79')
Japan later replaced Ueda with Goto after the fourth goal (85')
Tunisia’s defensive actions included sliding across to cover the right channel and clearing repeated corner pressure
key_matchups: Ito vs Ben Hmida in the right channel (77')
Ueda vs Dahmen and Tunisia’s defenders on the line (83')
Sano delivering to Ueda at the far post (83')
phases.summary: Japan controlled the phase through relentless industry, pressing Tunisia back and repeatedly creating danger from the right side and set-piece pressure. Tunisia’s occasional corner threat was largely repelled, while Japan’s crisp right-side move for Ueda’s 83rd-minute header ended the contest as a tactical one-way street.
